w:r.w:t: Each destination includes: Overview · Trip Details · Top Attractions · Travel Highlights · Day-wise Itinerary · Inclusions & Exclusions · Travel Tips · Image Suggestions · SEO Data

Bali is Indonesia's famed island of the gods — a lush tropical paradise where ancient Hindu temples meet terraced rice paddies, pristine beaches, and world-class surf. Known for its rich arts scene, vibrant nightlife in Seminyak and Kuta, and deeply spiritual culture, Bali seamlessly blends adventure with luxury. Whether you're seeking adrenaline-pumping water sports, serene temple visits, or a relaxing pool villa, Bali delivers an unforgettable experience for every type of traveller.
April – October (dry season). May–June is ideal — less crowded than July–August, with clear skies and perfect beach weather.
Uluwatu Temple — clifftop sea temple with legendary Kecak Fire Dance at sunset
Tanjung Benoa — premier water sports hub (jet ski, parasailing, banana boat)
Ulundanu Beratan Temple — iconic temple floating on crater lake in Bedugul
Tanah Lot — sacred sea temple perched on an offshore rock formation
Mount Batur & Kintamani — panoramic volcanic views with crater lake
Tegenungan Waterfall — lush jungle waterfall near Ubud
Alas Harum — cultural eco-park with rice terraces, swings, and art
Ayung River — premier white-water rafting corridor through gorge
Kecak Fire Dance at sunset — Uluwatu's most unmissable cultural performance
ATV tandem ride through Bali's countryside, jungle and muddy tracks
Stay in a private 1-bedroom pool villa for the last two nights
Jet ski and parasailing at Tanjung Benoa beach
Traditional airport welcome with flower garland
Private English-speaking driver throughout the tour
Airport pickup by private car. Hotel check-in with traditional welcome (flower garland, wet tissue, souvenir). 1 bottle of mineral water per person. Free time to relax and explore.
After breakfast: Jet Ski and Parasailing at Tanjung Benoa. Visit Uluwatu Temple perched on the cliff. Watch the legendary Kecak Fire Dance performance at sunset. Return to hotel.
After breakfast: Drive to Bedugul, visit Ulundanu Beratan Temple on the serene crater lake. Continue to iconic Tanah Lot sea temple — spectacular at sunset. Return to hotel.

After breakfast: Panoramic views of Mount Batur and Lake Batur at Kintamani. Visit Tegenungan Waterfall in the jungle. Explore Alas Harum cultural eco-park (entrance included, optional activities at own cost). Move to Pool Villa hotel.
After breakfast: White-water rafting on Ayung River through the lush gorge. ATV tandem ride through countryside, paddy fields, jungle, and muddy tracks. Return to Pool Villa to relax.
Breakfast at hotel. Check-out. Private transfer to Ngurah Rai International Airport as per flight schedule.
Visa on Arrival (~USD 35 / IDR 500,000 per pax)
All transfers and full-day tours (max 10 hrs/day)
Traditional airport welcome with flower garland
Wet tissue and souvenir upon arrival
1 bottle mineral water per person per day
Complete the Indonesian e-visa form at allindonesia.imigrasi.go.id at least 72 hours before arrival.
Carry a sarong for temple visits — many temples require modest dress. Some lend them at the entrance.
May is a great shoulder season: fewer crowds than peak July–August with similar weather.
Keep small Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) for tips, local warungs, and market shopping.
Best time to visit Tanah Lot and Uluwatu is at sunset — arrive 45 minutes early for a good spot.
Water sports at Tanjung Benoa are best in the morning when the sea is calmer.
Sunscreen, insect repellent, and a light rain jacket are must-packs for outdoor activities.
Uluwatu temple Bali cliff sunset orange sky
Bali rice terrace terraced fields Tegalalang aerial view
Tanah Lot temple sea ocean Bali
Tanjung Benoa parasailing water sports Bali beach
Bali pool villa infinity pool tropical resort
Tegenungan waterfall Bali jungle lush green
ATV ride Bali jungle mud adventure
Bali Tour Package 2026 | 5 Nights 6 Days | Temples, Beaches & Adventure | The Travel's Guru

Explore the best of Bali in 5 nights and 6 days. Includes Uluwatu sunset, Tanah Lot, water sports, Kintamani volcano views, white-water rafting, ATV ride, and private pool villa stay. Starting USD 725. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Bali tour package 2026, Bali 5 nights 6 days, Bali travel package India, Uluwatu temple tour, Tanah Lot Bali, Bali water sports, Kintamani tour, Bali pool villa package, Bali adventure trip, The Travel's Guru

The Andaman & Nicobar Islands are a tropical paradise in the Bay of Bengal — an archipelago of 572 emerald islands blessed with pristine white-sand beaches, turquoise lagoons, and some of Asia's finest coral reefs. From the historic Cellular Jail in Port Blair to the world-famous Radhanagar Beach on Havelock Island, and the secluded paradise of Neil Island, Andaman offers a perfect blend of history, adventure, and untouched natural beauty.
October – May (dry season). December–April is peak season with ideal diving and beach conditions.
Cellular Jail (Port Blair) — iconic colonial-era prison with emotional light & sound show
Radhanagar Beach (Havelock) — ranked among Asia's most beautiful beaches
Elephant Beach (Havelock) — snorkelling with vibrant coral and marine life
Kalapathar Beach — serene beach with unique black granite boulders
Neil Island — Natural Bridge, Bharatpur Beach, Lakshmanpur sunset
Corbyn's Cove Beach (Port Blair) — palm-fringed beach near the capital
Chidiyatapu (Port Blair) — 'Bird Island' famed for spectacular sunsets
Havelock Island Marine Life — rich corals, tropical fish, sea turtles
Cruise transfers between islands on premium ferries
Cellular Jail light & sound show — a powerful historical tribute
Private A/C vehicle for all transfers and sightseeing
Multi-island circuit: Port Blair → Havelock → Neil → Port Blair
24-hour customer care with a dedicated tour coordinator

Arrival at Veer Savarkar International Airport. Private transfer to hotel and check-in. Visit Corbyn's Cove beach — palm-fringed and scenic. After lunch, tour Cellular Jail. Evening: attend the iconic light & sound show depicting India's freedom struggle. Overnight in Port Blair.

Early breakfast and check-out. Ferry to Havelock Island. Visit the legendary Radhanagar Beach — pristine white sands, crystal-clear water, and lush forest backdrop. Ranked among the finest beaches in Asia. Overnight in Havelock.

Morning boat excursion to Elephant Beach (~40 mins). Snorkelling over rich coral reefs with tropical marine life. Optional activities: diving, kayaking, trekking (at extra cost). After lunch, visit Kalapathar Beach — sandy beach meeting black granite boulders. Overnight in Havelock.
Ferry to Neil Island (~1 hr). Check-in at resort. Evening sightseeing: Natural Bridge (a natural rock arch formation), Lakshmanpur Beach at sunset, and Bharatpur Beach. Overnight in Neil Island.
Ferry back to Port Blair. Remaining sightseeing covered. Visit Chidiyatapu — the southwestern tip of South Andaman, famous for its glorious sunset and bird-watching. Overnight in Port Blair.
Hotel check-out. Comfortable private transfer to airport. Fly home with cherished island memories.
Airfare to/from Port Blair (IXZ) and airport taxes
Entry fees at sightseeing spots (except Cellular Jail)
Private A/C vehicle (Xylo/Scorpio/similar) for all sightseeing
Adventure activities at beaches (diving, kayaking, ATV)
Entry fee: Cellular Jail + Light & Sound Show
Any costs due to natural calamities or strikes
24-hr customer care with dedicated tour coordinator
Tips for guides, drivers, and hotel staff
Driver allowance and all parking fees
Book inter-island ferries and hotels well in advance — Havelock gets fully booked in April.
Carry reef-safe sunscreen for snorkelling — regular sunscreen damages Andaman's fragile corals.
The light & sound show at Cellular Jail is deeply moving — bring a light jacket for the evening.
Neil Island is quieter and more laid-back than Havelock — perfect for a peaceful overnight stay.
Photography at Cellular Jail is restricted inside; check current rules on arrival.
Mobile network coverage is limited on Neil Island; inform family before departing Havelock.
April can get warm and humid — carry oral rehydration salts, light cotton clothing, and lots of water.
Radhanagar Beach Havelock Island Andaman turquoise water aerial
Cellular Jail Port Blair historical building India
Elephant Beach Andaman coral reef snorkelling underwater
Neil Island beach blue lagoon Andaman
Kalapathar beach Havelock black rocks sunset
Andaman Islands aerial view tropical islands Bay of Bengal
Andaman cruise ferry inter island transfer
Andaman Tour Package 2026 | 5 Nights 6 Days | Havelock, Neil Island & Port Blair | The Travel's Guru

Discover the Andaman Islands in 5 nights and 6 days. Multi-island circuit covering Port Blair, Havelock, and Neil Island. Includes Cellular Jail, Radhanagar Beach, snorkelling, cruises, and sightseeing. Starting INR 1,04,900. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Andaman tour package 2026, Andaman 5 nights 6 days, Havelock Neil Island tour, Andaman package from Surat, Cellular Jail Port Blair, Radhanagar Beach tour, Andaman snorkelling, Andaman island hopping, The Travel's Guru Andaman

Himachal Pradesh's golden triangle — Shimla, Kullu, and Manali — offers one of India's most iconic mountain road trips. Colonial-era Shimla enchants with its toy train, Mall Road, and Himalayan panoramas. Kullu delights with shawl markets and riverside orchards. Manali, the 'Valley of the Gods', stuns with snow-capped peaks, adventure sports, and ancient temples. From the snow line at Solang Valley to the mystical Hadimba Devi temple, this circuit packs extraordinary diversity into 6 days.
March–June for snow and pleasant weather. October–November for clear skies and autumn colours. Avoid monsoons (July–August) due to landslides.
Mall Road & Ridge (Shimla) — colonial heart of the hill capital
Snow Point Kufri — snow activities and panoramic Himalayan views
Christ Church & Jakhu Temple (Shimla) — heritage and spirituality
Kullu Shawl Factory — watch artisans weave famous Kullu shawls
Solang Valley Snow Point — skiing, horse riding, snow scooter, yak riding
Hadimba Devi Temple (Manali) — 16th-century cedar forest temple
Tibetan Monastery (Manali) — vibrant Himalayan Buddhist culture
Van Vihar National Park (Manali) — pine forest and river walk
Snow activities at Solang Valley — skiing, snow scooter, yak ride
Scenic drive through Kullu Valley along the Beas River
Colonial architecture and Himalayan sunsets from Shimla's Ridge
Private, personalized tour with dedicated vehicle throughout
Meet representative at Delhi Railway Station. Drive to Shimla (~8–9 hrs). Check-in at hotel. Evening stroll on Mall Road. Dinner and overnight in Shimla.

After breakfast: Snow Point at Kufri (skiing, horse/yak rides at own cost). Lakkar Bazaar for woodcraft shopping. Visit Christ Church, Mall Road, The Ridge, and Indira Gandhi Tourist Park. Sankat Mochan Temple and Jakhu Temple. Dinner and overnight in Shimla.

After breakfast, check-out. Drive toward Manali. En-route stop in Kullu: visit a shawl weaving factory to see local artisans at work. Continue to Manali (~4 hrs). Check-in at hotel. Evening leisure on Manali Mall Road. Dinner and overnight in Manali.

After breakfast: Drive to Solang Valley. Snow activities at snow line — skiing, horse riding, yak riding, snow scooter (at own expense). Return to Manali by afternoon. Leisure evening. Dinner and overnight in Manali.

After breakfast: Van Vihar National Park nature walk. Tibetan Monastery. Hadimba Devi Temple (16th-century shrine amid cedar forest). Manali Mall Road and Club House. Evening at leisure. Dinner and overnight in Manali.
After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Delhi Railway Station (~12–14 hrs). Transfer and departure with fond Himalayan memories.
Airfare or train fare to/from Delhi
Private vehicle (sedan/similar) for all transfers and sightseeing
Snow activity charges at Solang Valley (skiing, snow scooter, etc.)
All vehicle taxes, toll taxes, parking and permit fees
Book well in advance for the May–June season — Shimla and Manali hotels fill up fast.
Carry warm layers even in May/June — temperatures drop significantly at night in Manali.
Road conditions between Shimla and Manali can be slow — be prepared for a long but scenic drive.
The Rohtang Pass permit (beyond Solang Valley) requires an advance online booking at himachaltourism.gov.in.
Try local Himachali trout, siddu (steamed bread), and Kullu apple products.
Altitude acclimatization: Manali sits at ~2,050 m — rest on arrival day and avoid strenuous activity.
Manali Solang Valley snow mountains Himachal Pradesh
Shimla Mall Road colonial buildings hill station India
Hadimba Devi temple cedar forest Manali
Kullu valley Beas River Himachal autumn leaves
Kufri snow point Shimla Himalayan panorama
Tibetan monastery Manali prayer flags mountain
Himachal Pradesh road trip mountain hairpin turns aerial
Shimla Manali Tour Package 2026 | 5 Nights 6 Days | Snow, Adventure & Himalayas | The Travel's Guru

Explore Shimla, Kullu, and Manali in 5 nights and 6 days from Delhi. Includes Solang Valley snow activities, Hadimba temple, Kufri, Mall Road, private transfers, and daily meals. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Shimla Manali tour package 2026, Himachal Pradesh trip, Manali Shimla 5 nights 6 days, Solang Valley snow, Kufri sightseeing, Shimla Kullu Manali itinerary, Himachal tour from Delhi, The Travel's Guru Himachal

This classic South Indian circuit pairs the royal grandeur of Mysore — Karnataka's city of palaces — with the cool misty heights of Ooty (Udhagamandalam) in the Nilgiris. Mysore dazzles with its illuminated maharaja's palace, manicured Brindavan Gardens, and vibrant street markets. Ooty seduces with its colonial Raj charm, emerald tea gardens, Doddabetta Peak, and the meandering toy train through forested ghats. A perfect blend of culture, nature, and fresh mountain air.
October – June. March–May is ideal for Ooty's pleasant weather. Avoid July–September (monsoon).
Mysore Palace (Amba Vilas) — India's most-visited heritage palace, spectacular when illuminated
Chamundi Hills — hilltop Chamundeshwari temple with panoramic views
Brindavan Gardens — spectacular terraced garden with musical fountain
St. Philomena's Cathedral — one of India's largest neo-Gothic cathedrals
Doddabetta Peak — highest point in the Nilgiris at 2,637 m
Ooty Botanical Garden — 55-acre Victorian-era garden with fossil tree
Pykara Lake & Waterfalls — serene Nilgiri highlands scenery
Ooty Rose Garden — Asia's largest rose garden with 20,000+ varieties
Mysore Palace — architecture and illumination on Sunday evenings
Scenic drive through Bandipur National Park (elephant sightings possible)
Nilgiri Mountain Railway (Toy Train) — UNESCO World Heritage ride
Tea and rose garden exploration in the Nilgiri highlands
Karanji Lake and Mysore Zoo — great for families

Arrive at Bengaluru Airport/Railway Station. Meet representative and proceed to Mysuru (3.5 hrs). Check-in and relax. Afternoon sightseeing: Mysore Palace, Chamundi Hills, St. Philomena's Cathedral, Brindavan Gardens. Overnight in Mysore.
After breakfast: Mysore Zoo (one of India's finest), Jaganmohan Palace Art Gallery, Karanji Lake nature walk, local market visit for silk sarees and sandalwood. Overnight in Mysore.
After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Ooty via scenic ghat roads passing Bandipur National Park (wildlife drive-through). On arrival, visit Ooty Lake and Botanical Garden. Check-in. Overnight in Ooty.

After breakfast: Doddabetta Peak (highest in Nilgiris — telescope house). Rose Garden Ooty (Asia's largest). Government Botanical Garden (fossil tree). Pykara Lake and Pykara Waterfalls. Overnight in Ooty.
After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Coimbatore Airport/Railway Station (3 hrs). Departure and onward journey.
Private A/C Ertiga for all transfers and sightseeing
Entrance fees at all sightseeing locations
Driver allowance, toll, and parking charges
Lunch and dinner (not included in meal plan)
5% GST included in total package cost
Any services not listed in inclusions
Book Mysore Palace entry tickets online to avoid long queues — the Sunday evening illumination (7–8 PM) is spectacular.
The Nilgiri Mountain Railway toy train from Mettupalayam to Ooty must be booked well in advance — seats sell out fast.
Ooty can be cold even in March — carry a light jacket, especially for Doddabetta Peak.
Drive through Bandipur in the early morning for the best wildlife sightings.
Try Ooty's famous homemade chocolate and fresh tea from the Nilgiri plantations.
Pykara Lake boat rides and Botanical Garden entry have separate tickets — carry small change.
Mysore Palace illuminated night Karnataka India
Ooty tea gardens Nilgiris green hills aerial view
Doddabetta Peak Nilgiris misty mountains Tamil Nadu
Brindavan Gardens Mysore fountain terraced garden
Ooty Rose Garden colourful roses bloom
Nilgiri Mountain Railway toy train blue mountains Tamil Nadu
Bandipur National Park elephant jeep safari Karnataka
Mysore Ooty Tour Package 2026 | 4 Nights 5 Days | Palaces, Hills & Gardens | The Travel's Guru

Explore Mysore and Ooty in 4 nights and 5 days. Includes Mysore Palace, Chamundi Hills, Ooty Botanical Garden, Doddabetta Peak, Pykara Lake, and private A/C car throughout. INR 66,600 inclusive of GST. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Mysore Ooty tour package 2026, Mysore Ooty 4 nights 5 days, South India tour package, Mysore palace tour, Ooty hill station trip, Nilgiris tour India, Bangalore Mysore Ooty itinerary, The Travel's Guru South India

Kashmir — described by Mughal emperors as heaven on earth — is India's most breathtaking destination. The emerald waters of Dal Lake with its ornate houseboats, the meadow paradise of Sonmarg, the florally carpeted Gulmarg, and the shepherd's valley of Pahalgam together create an experience of unparalleled beauty. Ancient Mughal gardens, saffron fields, Himalayan glaciers, and the warmth of Kashmiri hospitality make this destination utterly unforgettable in any season.
April–June for lush meadows and tulip blooms. December–February for snow and skiing in Gulmarg. October–November for golden autumn foliage.
Srinagar → Sonmarg → Srinagar → Gulmarg → Pahalgam → Srinagar
Dal Lake, Gulmarg Gondola, Sonmarg Glacier, Pahalgam Valley
Dal Lake (Srinagar) — iconic shikhara boat rides past floating gardens and ornate houseboats
Mughal Gardens — Nishat Bagh and Shalimar Bagh, terraced gardens above the lake
Shankaracharya Temple — ancient hilltop shrine with panoramic Srinagar views
Sonmarg (Meadow of Gold) — alpine meadows, Sindh River, and Thajiwas Glacier
Gulmarg (Meadow of Flowers) — world's highest operating cable car (Gondola Phase 2)
Pahalgam (Valley of Shepherds) — Betaab Valley, Aru Valley, and Lidder River
Awantipora Ruins — 9th-century Shiva temple ruins en-route to Pahalgam
Chashmeshahi — the smallest of the Mughal gardens, fed by a natural spring
Shikara ride on Dal Lake — quintessential Kashmir experience
Gulmarg Gondola — Asia's highest and longest cable car, Phase 1 & 2
Sonmarg meadows — snow points and pony ride to Thajiwas Glacier
Pahalgam's Betaab Valley and Aru Valley — stunning alpine landscapes
Kashmiri cuisine: Rogan Josh, Yakhni, Kashmiri Pulao, Kahwa tea
Shopping: dry fruits, Pashmina shawls, Kashmiri carpets, saffron

Arrival at Srinagar Airport. Meet & greet by representative. Transfer to hotel/houseboat and check-in. Evening at leisure. Optional: Shikara ride on Dal Lake at sunset. Dinner and overnight in Srinagar.

Breakfast at hotel. Full-day excursion to Sonmarg (Meadow of Gold) — scenic drive along the Sindh River through pine forests. Optional pony ride to Thajiwas Glacier and snow points (at own cost, subject to weather). Return to Srinagar by evening. Dinner and overnight in Srinagar.

After breakfast: Mughal Gardens — Nishat Bagh and Shalimar Bagh. Chashme Shahi garden. Shankaracharya Temple with panoramic city views. Afternoon: shopping for Pashmina shawls, Kashmiri dry fruits, and saffron. Evening at Dal Lake promenade. Dinner and overnight in Srinagar.

After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Gulmarg (Meadow of Flowers, ~2 hrs). Check-in at hotel. Afternoon: explore the gorgeous meadow and enjoy snow views. Optional: Gondola Cable Car Phase 1 or 2 — Asia's highest cable car (at own cost). Dinner and overnight in Gulmarg.

After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Pahalgam (Valley of Shepherds, ~4 hrs). En-route: Awantipora Ruins — ancient 9th-century Shiva temple and seasonal saffron fields (October). Check-in at hotel. Evening: walk by the Lidder River. Dinner and overnight in Pahalgam.

After breakfast, check-out. Local sightseeing in Pahalgam by union cab (at own cost): Betaab Valley, Aru Valley, and Chandanwari. Drive back to Srinagar Airport for departure. Tour ends with beautiful Kashmir memories.
Airfare to/from Srinagar and airport taxes
Shikara ride on Dal Lake (optional, at own cost)
Private vehicle for all transfers and sightseeing
Gulmarg Gondola Cable Car (Phase 1 & 2) at own cost
Pony rides and snow activities at Sonmarg
Meet & greet by local representative at airport
Pahalgam local union cab for Betaab/Aru Valley
Entry fees at Mughal Gardens and other sights
Book Gulmarg Gondola tickets online in advance at gulmarggondola.com — Phase 2 (Apharwat Peak) offers dramatic views.
April–May is the tulip blooming season in Srinagar — witness Asia's largest tulip garden (Indira Gandhi Memorial Tulip Garden) in full bloom.
Pahalgam's Betaab and Aru Valleys require a local union cab — negotiate the rate with the union at the main stand.
Kashmiri houseboats on Dal Lake are an iconic experience — opt for a houseboat for at least one night in Srinagar.
Carry warm layers in all seasons — Gulmarg and Sonmarg can be cold even in June.
Purchase saffron and Pashmina only from certified government emporiums or reputed shops to ensure authenticity.
Dal Lake Srinagar houseboat shikara sunset Kashmir
Gulmarg meadow snow Himalayan mountains Kashmir India
Sonmarg glacier meadow river Kashmir mountains
Nishat Bagh Mughal garden Dal Lake Srinagar
Pahalgam Betaab Valley river green meadow Kashmir
Gulmarg gondola cable car Apharwat Peak snow
Srinagar old city market shawl Pashmina Kashmir
Kashmir Tour Package 2026 | 5 Nights 6 Days | Dal Lake, Gulmarg & Pahalgam | The Travel's Guru
Discover Kashmir in 5 nights and 6 days. Covers Srinagar, Sonmarg, Gulmarg, and Pahalgam. Includes Dal Lake shikara ride, Mughal Gardens, and scenic Himalayan drives. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Kashmir tour package 2026, Kashmir 5 nights 6 days, Srinagar Gulmarg Pahalgam tour, Dal Lake houseboat, Kashmir trip from Surat, Gulmarg gondola, Sonmarg tour, Kashmir travel package India, The Travel's Guru Kashmir

Kerala — God's Own Country — is India's most romantically diverse destination. Munnar's endless tea gardens and mist-draped peaks, Thekkady's spice plantations and tiger reserve, and Alleppey's tranquil backwater network aboard a traditional houseboat together offer an experience that is simultaneously wild, peaceful, and deeply cultural. Add Kathakali dance, Kalaripayattu martial arts, Periyar wildlife, and the freshest seafood in the country, and Kerala is the ultimate multi-sensory escape.
September – March (post-monsoon and winter). October–February is ideal for houseboats and wildlife. Avoid heavy monsoon (June–August) for outdoor activities.
MAP (Breakfast + Dinner) at hotels; all meals on houseboat
Munnar Tea Gardens — rolling hills of emerald tea bushes and misty viewpoints
Mattupetty Dam & Indo-Swiss Dairy Farm — scenic lake boating
Eravikulam National Park — habitat of the endangered Nilgiri Tahr
Periyar Lake Boat Ride — spot elephants, deer, and birds from the water
Periyar Tiger Reserve — one of India's finest wildlife sanctuaries
Alleppey (Alappuzha) Backwaters — glide through paddy fields and coconut groves on a houseboat
Kathakali Dance Show (Thekkady) — elaborate classical dance of Kerala
Kalaripayattu Show — ancient Indian martial art, origin of Asian combat
Overnight on a traditional Kerala houseboat (kettuvallam) with all meals included
Periyar Lake boat safari — chance of elephant sightings from the water
Cheeyapara and Vallara Waterfalls on the drive to Munnar
Kathakali and Kalaripayattu cultural performances in Thekkady
Eravikulam National Park — home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr
Arrival at Cochin Airport/Railway Station. Meet tour manager and drive to Munnar (4 hrs). En-route: visit Cheeyapara and Vallara Waterfalls. Check-in at hotel. Overnight in Munnar.

After breakfast: Mattupetty Dam and boating on the reservoir. Shooting Point and Echo Point. Tea garden visit — walk through emerald rows of Camellia sinensis. Blossom Park. Tea and handicraft shopping in Munnar town. Overnight in Munnar.

After breakfast, check-out. Visit Eravikulam National Park / Rajamalai — home to the rare Nilgiri Tahr. Visit a spice plantation (pepper, cardamom, clove). Drive to Thekkady (Land of Tigers, 4–5 hrs). Check-in. Overnight in Thekkady.

After breakfast: Boat ride on Periyar Lake — spot elephants, sambar, and birds. Optional Gavi Village Jeep Safari (at own cost). Evening cultural shows: Kathakali Dance and Kalaripayattu Martial Arts. Spice shopping in local market. Overnight in Thekkady.

After breakfast, check-out. Drive to Alleppey — Venice of the East (4–5 hrs). 12:00 PM: check-in to private houseboat. Cruise through the backwaters of Kerala — paddy fields, coconut palms, village life, and migratory birds. Lunch, evening tea, and dinner served on board. Stunning sunset from the houseboat deck. Overnight on houseboat.
Morning backwater cruise with breakfast on board. 9:00 AM: check-out from houseboat. Drive to Cochin Airport/Railway Station (2 hrs). Tour ends with beautiful Kerala memories.
Entry fees at all sightseeing places
Private A/C Ertiga for all transfers and sightseeing
Adventure activities and optional boat rides
Coimbatore/Cochin drop at end of tour
Meals not mentioned (lunch at hotels on Days 1–5)
Fuel, toll, parking, and driver allowance
Gavi Village Jeep Safari (optional, own cost)
Book the houseboat well in advance for May — houseboat demand peaks in holiday season.
AC on houseboats is generally available only 9 PM–6 AM — carry light breathable clothing.
Eravikulam National Park may restrict entry during calving season (Jan–Mar) — check ahead.
Periyar boat rides are available in the early morning (7 AM) and afternoon — morning is best for wildlife.
Carry cash for spice market shopping in Thekkady — many vendors don't accept cards.
Kerala's backwaters are most beautiful in the golden light of early morning or late afternoon.
Try authentic Kerala Sadhya (banana leaf feast) at a local restaurant in Alleppey.
Kerala houseboat backwaters Alleppey coconut palms sunset
Munnar tea garden green hills mist Kerala aerial
Periyar Lake boat ride elephant Kerala wildlife
Kathakali dance performance colorful costume Kerala
Eravikulam National Park Nilgiri Tahr mountains
Alleppey backwaters paddy fields water reflection Kerala
Thekkady spice plantation cardamom pepper Kerala
Kerala Tour Package 2026 | 5 Nights 6 Days | Munnar, Thekkady & Alleppey Houseboat | The Travel's Guru

Explore Kerala in 5 nights and 6 days. Covers Munnar, Thekkady, and Alleppey backwaters. Includes houseboat stay, Periyar wildlife, Kathakali show, and private car. Starting INR 23,850/person. Book with The Travel's Guru.

Kerala tour package 2026, Kerala 5 nights 6 days, Munnar Thekkady Alleppey trip, Kerala houseboat package, Periyar wildlife tour, God's Own Country tour, Kerala package from Surat, The Travel's Guru Kerala

Goa is India's most beloved coastal escape — a sun-soaked stretch of the Konkan coast where Portuguese heritage meets Bollywood vibes, golden beaches, and a year-round festive spirit. From the lively beach shacks and beach parties of North Goa to the secluded coves and historic churches of South Goa, the state has something for everyone. Whether you're a heritage enthusiast, a party-goer, a water sports lover, or simply someone who wants to relax with a coconut by the sea, Goa never disappoints.
November – February (ideal beach weather). October–November and February–March for fewer crowds and good prices.
Calangute & Baga Beach (North Goa) — Goa's most popular beach with lively shacks
Anjuna Beach & Flea Market — bohemian beach vibes and Wednesday flea market
Fort Aguada (North Goa) — 17th-century Portuguese fort overlooking the Arabian Sea
Candolim Beach — quieter and more upscale beach near the hotel
Basilica of Bom Jesus (South Goa) — UNESCO heritage church with St. Francis Xavier's remains
Se Cathedral (South Goa) — one of Asia's largest Gothic cathedrals
Dudhsagar Waterfalls — magnificent four-tiered waterfall on the Goa–Karnataka border
North Goa sightseeing: Calangute, Baga, Anjuna, Fort Aguada, and Vagator
South Goa heritage: Basilica of Bom Jesus, Se Cathedral, and Old Goa churches
Complimentary swimming pool at Aldeia Santa Rita
Welcome drink and daily breakfast at the hotel
Private A/C sedan for all airport transfers and sightseeing

Arrival at Goa Airport. Private A/C sedan transfer to Aldeia Santa Rita, Candolim. Check-in, welcome drink, and freshening up. North Goa sightseeing: Calangute Beach, Baga Beach, Fort Aguada, Anjuna, Vagator cliffs. Leisure evening at Candolim Beach. Overnight at hotel.

Breakfast at hotel. South Goa sightseeing: Basilica of Bom Jesus (UNESCO), Se Cathedral, Church of St. Francis of Assisi, Panjim Old Quarter (Fontainhas), Miramar Beach. Optional: spice plantation tour or dolphin watching cruise (at own cost). Overnight at hotel.
Breakfast at hotel. Check-out. Private transfer to Goa Airport/Railway Station for onward journey.
Airfare or train fare to/from Goa
Lunch and dinner (not included in meal plan)
North Goa sightseeing by private A/C sedan
Water sports, dolphin cruise, spice plantation (optional, own cost)
South Goa sightseeing by private A/C sedan
Airport pickup and drop by private A/C sedan
April is warm in Goa (30–35°C) — carry light cotton clothing, sunscreen SPF 50+, and sunglasses.
Rent a scooter or two-wheeler for flexible beach-hopping in Goa — it is the most popular local way to explore.
Visit Basilica of Bom Jesus early morning to avoid tour groups and heat.
The Wednesday flea market at Anjuna is great for shopping, but April may be off-season — check dates.
Candolim is a calmer, more family-friendly stretch than Calangute or Baga.
Try Goan specialties: fish curry rice, prawn balchão, bebinca dessert, and cold King's beer.
